data.output:
ID:       ddx-63155d5c
Title:    feat(escalation): auto-reframe + auto-decompose on persistent failure (no human-in-loop)
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability
Owner:    erik
Desc:     PROBLEM
  Today, when a bead persistently fails (ladder exhausted on
  multiple drain cycles, repair cycles exhausted, or
  review_spec_gap / review_too_large fires), the only path is
  status=proposed → operator action.

  ADR-024 P5 (no human-in-the-loop, sister bead rank 19) requires
  automatic recovery: before parking to proposed, the system must
  attempt automatic reframe (rewrite description + AC) AND/OR
  decompose (split into child beads). Only if both fail does the
  bead reach proposed.

  Today only intake-time reframe (PreClaimIntakeActionableButRewritten
  in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go) can rewrite the bead.
  Post-failure reframe does not exist. Decomposition only fires
  for review_too_large, not for "implementer keeps failing"
  patterns.

CURRENT STATE
  - cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake.go: intake can apply
    actionable_but_rewritten edits. ONE-shot, before claim.
  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has hooks like
    PostAttemptDecompositionHook for review_too_large but NOT a
    general post-ladder-exhaustion hook.
  - status=proposed is the default escape hatch for spec gaps,
    too-large, ladder-exhausted, repair-cycle-exhausted.
  - No consecutive_ladder_exhaustions counter; the system can't
    distinguish "first ladder exhaustion" from "third".
  - No reframer or decomposer prompt templates exist.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a new post-failure recovery pipeline. After ladder exhaustion
  triggers consecutive_ladder_exhaustions >= 2 (per TD-031 §6 new
  field, sister bead rank 19):

  1. Determine the failure class:
     - review_spec_gap, review_missing_acceptance → REFRAME path.
     - review_too_large or repeated execution_failed at top tier →
       DECOMPOSE path.
     - Other persistent execution_failed → REFRAME first, then if
       reframe doesn't help on next cycle, DECOMPOSE.

  2. Operator opt-out: if bead has label "recovery:manual",
     skip auto-recovery entirely and park to status=proposed
     (matches ADR-024 Operator escape hatches subsection).

  3. REFRAME path:
     - Add prompt template at library/prompts/agents/reframer.md
       — defines the reframer role's input contract (bead body +
       failure history) and output contract (structured edits to
       description and AC).
     - Dispatch a strong-tier (smart+) agent with role=reframer.
     - Apply the edits via the same actionable_but_rewritten path

ID:       ddx-e012af90
Title:    feat(escalation): add PostLadderExhaustionHook type, wiring, routing, and recovery:manual bypass
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability
Parent:   ddx-63155d5c
Desc:     PROBLEM
  execute_bead_loop.go increments consecutiveLadderExhaustionsKey when per-bead
  budget exhausts (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1459), but when the
  counter reaches >= 2 there is no PostLadderExhaustionHook to dispatch
  auto-recovery. The recovery:manual label opt-out does not exist. Failure-class
  routing (spec_gap → REFRAME; too_large → DECOMPOSE; persistent execution_failed
  → REFRAME-first) is also absent.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1459 calls
  incrementConsecutiveLadderExhaustions but the comment marks the subsequent
  hook as "sister bead ddx-63155d5c" which was never implemented.
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2847 (applyRepairCycleExhaustedEscalation)
ID:       ddx-ca3e0d37
Title:    feat(escalation): implement REFRAME path with reframer.md template and agent dispatch
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability
Parent:   ddx-63155d5c
Deps:     ddx-e012af90
Desc:     PROBLEM
  The PostLadderExhaustionHook infrastructure (parent child ddx-e012af90) routes
  SpecGap and PersistentExecutionFailed failure classes to a REFRAME path, but no
  reframer prompt template and no reframer dispatch logic exist. When the hook
  fires for these classes, it must dispatch a strong-tier agent to rewrite the
  bead's description and AC, then apply those edits via the existing
  actionable_but_rewritten path in cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_rewrite.go.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has no reframer dispatch code.
  library/prompts/agents/reframer.md does not exist.
  The intake actionable_but_rewritten apply path exists but is only called
ID:       ddx-60bc5e9a
Title:    feat(escalation): implement DECOMPOSE path with decomposer.md template and agent dispatch
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability
Parent:   ddx-63155d5c
Deps:     ddx-e012af90
Desc:     PROBLEM
  The PostLadderExhaustionHook infrastructure (ddx-e012af90) routes TooLarge
  failure class to a DECOMPOSE path, but no decomposer prompt template and no
  decomposer dispatch logic exist. When the hook fires for TooLarge (or as a
  fallback from reframe failure on PersistentExecutionFailed), it must dispatch
  a strong-tier agent to produce 2-5 child bead specs, then file them via
  Store.Create with the parent linked.

ROOT CAUSE
  cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go has PostAttemptDecompositionHook for
  the no_changes/orchestrator_action:decompose path, but no general decomposer
  for post-ladder-exhaustion scenarios.
ID:       ddx-9b080448
Title:    feat(escalation): add recovery circuit breaker, escape hatch, and integration tests
Type:     task
Status:   open
Priority: 0
Labels:   phase:6, area:agent, kind:feature, spec:ADR-024, reliability
Parent:   ddx-63155d5c
Deps:     ddx-ca3e0d37, ddx-60bc5e9a
Desc:     PROBLEM
  With reframe (ddx-ca3e0d37) and decompose (ddx-60bc5e9a) paths in place, two
  gaps remain:
  1. No circuit breaker: recovery hooks can run multiple times accumulating
     unbounded cost before either succeeds or falls through to proposed.
  2. No escape hatch wiring: when BOTH reframe and decompose fail (or are
     skipped), the bead must park to status=proposed with an auto-recovery-failed
     event. PersistentExecutionFailed class must try reframe first, then if
     reframe fails, try decompose; only then escape.
  3. Per-bead budget interaction: if the per-bead budget exhausts during a
     recovery attempt, it must fall through to per_bead_budget_exhausted (not
     status=proposed).
